stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), d ≠ [] → Even (Delta.countReft d) → Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: The refutation exploited only the degenerate even word `[]`, whose fixed-point side is automatically inhabited. This candidate asks whether removing exactly that obstruction restores the even-to-Henkin bridge, making the boundary exposed by the counterexample precise rather than merely local.
priority_rationale: If true, this would isolate the verified even-side failure to the degenerate identity case and would immediately rewrite the current theory summary.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft d) → (Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ↔ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α))
priority_rationale: This is a family-level odd-parity existence criterion for arbitrary composites, directly addressing a central missing summary theorem.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] (d : Delta) (x : α), Odd (Delta.countReft d) → x ≐ Delta.act d x → x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
priority_rationale: A canonical-form result for odd fixed points is reusable and helps compress the odd classification path, though it is still a support theorem rather than the full classification.

stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] (d : Delta) (x y : α), Odd (Delta.countReft d) → x ≐ Delta.act d x → y ≐ Delta.act d y → x ≐ y
priority_rationale: Odd-side uniqueness would be a real structural step toward classification, but by itself it does not settle existence or connect fully to the Gödel bridge.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α), Nonempty (ACR α) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.Prov α) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.Reft α) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.APS α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: The verified counterexample shows that bare APS does not force Henkin fixed points, but it uses an infinite model. A finite counterexample would isolate infiniteness as inessential and materially advance the counterexample-construction agenda by identifying a sharper structural boundary.
priority_rationale: A finite counterexample would sharply advance the finite-versus-infinite regime question, though it does not by itself characterize composite-level parity behavior.

stmt: ∃ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), d ≠ [] ∧ Even (Delta.countReft d) ∧ Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
summary_delta: If true, this would show that the failure of the even-parity criterion is not just an artifact of the identity composite, but a genuine family-level separation between even fixed points and Henkin existence. That would be a stronger and more reusable obstruction story than the current `d = []` counterexample alone.
priority_rationale: A nondegenerate even counterexample would materially sharpen the boundary story, but it is still an existential obstruction rather than a corrected family-level criterion.
